# Backlog Item: BI-260051 - Implement Platform Token Handoff via URL Parameter

- **Date**: 2026-06-10
- **Status**: Done

## Description

When the Crystord platform page (`crystord.com`) redirects a signed-in user to the workspace app (`app.crystord.com`), the app should not require the user to sign in again. Because the two pages are on different origins, `localStorage` is not shared. The platform must pass the token in the redirect URL as `?token=<jwt>`, and the app must consume it on startup.

This backlog item implements the token handoff: a `useUrlTokenHandoff` hook reads the `token` query parameter, calls `signIn`, and strips the parameter from the URL via `history.replaceState`.

## Goals

- On startup, detect a `?token=` parameter in the URL.
- If present, sign the user in with that token (persisted to `localStorage`).
- Immediately strip the parameter from the URL so it does not linger in browser history.
- The handoff must apply even if a previous session already exists in `localStorage`.
- 100% unit test coverage for the new hook and its integration in `AuthProvider`.

## Related Artifacts

- **ADR**: `ADR-260052`
- **Requirements**: `REQ-FR-260062`, `REQ-CR-260021`, `REQ-OR-260015`
